name: Bill Pemberton
abstract: William McLane, better known as Bill Pemberton (March 5, 1918 in New York City – December 13, 1984 in New York City) was an American jazz double-bassist. Pemberton played violin as a child before switching to bass.

works.t: A to Z: And Her Mother Came Too!
If I Had a Talking Picture of You
Fifty Million Frenchmen: I Worship You
Fifty Million Frenchmen: You Do Something to Me
Right This Way: I'll Be Seeing You

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: JPJ Quartet

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: All of these people played in the festival. We had Bill Pemberton, a marvelous bass player... myself on saxophones and I believe that was the band.

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Pemberton played bass, of all people... We had a hell of an 8-piece band.

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: Two years ago I took Johnny Letman on a tour for Honestier, and we had Ronnie Cole on the drums, we had George Kelly, we had Bill Pemberton, and I played the piano

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Benjamin... came from the school of George Duvivier and Milt Hinton... Bill Pemberton. And that's pretty good company to be in as a bassist.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Hall
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah, Bill Pemberton used to play one of those. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...my old dear friend Bill Pemberton walked in and wants to sit in on the session. [INTERVIEWEE] Well you know how it is, into each rain a little life must fall.
